sources.notes: Official Educational Round 186 editorial page; used with the provided official solution code to confirm the frequency-maintained hard version.

reference_candidates.notes: 洛谷题解候选来源；只有实际参考后才移动到 sources。

annotation.summary: Maintain the same reindeer subset-count query as F1 under updates to the available multiset.
annotation.constraints: The hard version has many online updates, so per-query work must depend only on the number of binary levels, not on the number of reindeer.
annotation.core_idea: Keep frequency counts of available reindeer by binary level and precompute factorials, inverse factorials, and powers of two. For each query target x, compute the same binary-carry requirement vector as in the easy version. Sweep levels from high to low: add all subsets that first exceed the requirement at the current level, multiply by the equal-count choice C(freq_i, req_i), and let all lower levels be free through powers of two. Updates only increment/decrement one frequency bucket.
annotation.complexity: O(B) per update/query after O(N+B) precomputation for B binary levels.

solution_variants.name: main
solution_variants.summary: Maintain level frequencies and answer each target by the descending lexicographic subset-count formula with precomputed combinations.

tags.evidence: The same carry-normalized requirement vector from F1 is the core target representation.
Each query counts valid subsets of a dynamic multiset by frequency class.
The per-level equal/exceed choices are computed with combinations and powers of two.
The dynamic multiset can be represented as frequency buckets keyed by level.
The target and levels are processed by binary bits.
